title: dianthus × laucheanus
intro: Dianthus × laucheanus, also known as the sweet william, is a flowering plant in the carnation family native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain and swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. Dianthus × laucheanus is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and can also be used in topical applications such as salves or ointments.
